rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly to justify a core modeling element in the citing study: explicit pre-, current-, and postpromotion dynamics, including discount-dependent prepromotion dips. In the citing paper, this is not a one-off background mention; it appears in conceptual framing, model specification, and interpretation of findings (e.g., why postpromotion dips may not appear with a short lag window). Thus, the cited work meaningfully informs how the citing paper structures and explains its promotion-response analysis.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Van Heerde et al. (2000) as a key empirical and conceptual basis for modeling promotion dynamics, especially pre- and postpromotion dip effects. It builds its loyalty-member versus nonmember revenue decomposition on top of that dynamic-effects foundation.
